scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: The scene's core interaction — ticking a 'Which areas can you cover?' checkbox that blocks submit — is not in the capture. The captured page is the public solicitation DETAIL page (/solicitations/4495/), which shows a READ-ONLY coverage panel; the actual selector lives on the /respond/ form. The capture stops one click short of the feature the scene is about.
fix_recommendation: Re-point this scene's capture to /solicitations/4495/respond/ and capture the coverage-checkbox selector (blocked-submit state + ticked state). Alternative: rewrite the scene narration to not claim a tick/blocked-submit action on the detail frame — but that abandons the scene's differentiating bet, so re-pointing the capture is the right fix.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: Narration says Amina 'ticks that exact coverage area on the application form' and the declared feature blocks submit until one area is chosen — neither the tickable checklist nor the blocked-submit is present in the captured footage (advisory).
fix_recommendation: Same as the design_soundness finding: re-point scene 3 to the /respond/ form so the demonstrated UI matches the narration.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The scene's load-bearing 'Coverage areas (from micro-plan)' panel is the least prominent thing on the page — an unstyled faint strip with tiny gray run-on text, no card, no icon, inverted against the bold iconned cards around it. A planner doing the 5-second scan reads 'generic create form', not 'my plan is already attached'.
fix_recommendation: Promote the 'Coverage areas (from micro-plan)' panel to a first-class card with the same visual weight as the other sections: bold iconned card header, card chrome, and the R6 plan rendered as a prominent chip with real typographic weight on '840 work areas · 2 wards'. Label the 'comparison, intervention' tokens (e.g. 'Study arms:'). Consider rendering the snapshotted coverage as a ConnectMap so the owner can visually confirm the exact area.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: The 'Create solicitation' hero action is the 7th element down the left rail, below 5 collapsed config accordions, styled as a near-twin of a 'Download Connect import CSV' outlined pill. The 5-second read lands on the amber 'out of tolerance' comparability card, not the hero action.
fix_recommendation: Promote 'Create solicitation' to the visually dominant PRIMARY action on a finished micro-plan (primary button, raised in the rail or in a plan-header action bar), distinct from the secondary 'Download CSV'.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: The hero CTA is a low-prominence outlined pill buried 7th in the rail; the arm-comparability card out-weights it; PLAN METRICS labels are a flat uppercase row.
fix_recommendation: Promote the 'Create solicitation' CTA to a primary button with clear visual hierarchy over secondary/export actions, and give the plan-review header a stronger title/identity treatment.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: The firm's selected-coverage pill 'R6 — Attakar × Gura' is textually identical to the page title, so the load-bearing 'firm's chosen area resolved from the snapshot' reads as an echo of the solicitation, not an independent selection. Comparative chrome ('Compare all applicants side by side', 'Review All (1 responses)') is dead weight at n=1.
fix_recommendation: Differentiate the firm's selected coverage from the solicitation it answers — show a snapshot-provenance marker (e.g. 'Selected · 2 wards') so the concept is visible not asserted. Collapse/reframe the comparative chrome when there is a single response.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: The destructive 'Award' action sits as an undifferentiated equal-weight inline link beside View/Review; the 'Compare all applicants' CTA leads nowhere at n=1.
fix_recommendation: Give the 'Award' action distinct, non-peer weight (or confirm-gate it) versus View/Review; hide comparative chrome until n>1.

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: The honesty beat re-uses scene 4's exact frame; the planning-vs-execution boundary message is 100% narration-dependent and the frame reads as a duplicate. The page does NOT overclaim auto-provisioning (good/honest), but the boundary is not made legible on-screen.
fix_recommendation: Options: (a) re-target the scene to a frame showing a 'Next: stand up the live Connect opportunity (coming soon)' affordance; (b) give 'Award' an inline 'what happens next' note naming live-opportunity provisioning as the deferred step; (c) keep the frame and add a deck callout framing Award as the shipped/next-step boundary (accepts concept_clarity capped at 3).

scene: 5 — Maya looks ahead to standing up the real opportunity
detail: 'Review All (1 responses)' grammar; scenes 4 and 5 reuse the identical frame (non-advancing, 'didn't I just see this?').
fix_recommendation: Fix '(1 responses)' -> '(1 response)'; suppress the 'Compare all applicants side by side' CTA when there is exactly one applicant.
